quick_wins: Delete 454 stale branches across 132 projects — zero risk, instant cleanup
Add justification for 5 projects with ≤5 unjustified false positives each — quick audit fix

ai_insights.narrative: The portfolio spans 198 repositories on SonarQube. The most significant concerns are around lifecycle and branch hygiene: 61 repositories are inactive and have not been scanned in over one year, and 132 repositories carry non-main branches that have not been scanned in more than 90 days. Configuration gaps affect 11 repositories, limiting analysis visibility across the portfolio. Quality gate compliance is broadly consistent, with only four repositories using non-compliant gates, though all three repositories with accepted issues lack justification comments, weakening auditability. Rule profile hygiene also requires attention, as five custom profiles do not inherit from built-in profiles and will need manual maintenance to remain current. Prioritising inactive project cleanup, stale branch removal, and configuration gap resolution would have the most immediate impact on portfolio hygiene.

sections.inactive_insights: 61 repositories are inactive and have not been scanned in over one year. This includes 15 repositories inactive for 1-2 years, 19 inactive for 2-4 years, and 27 inactive for more than four years. Retaining unused repositories increases maintenance overhead and can distort portfolio metrics.
sections.inactive_action: Request project POCs to review these repositories for continued relevance, with inactive ones to be removed or archived from the SonarQube instance to optimise maintenance and maintain a clear project portfolio.
sections.duplication_insights: One repository exceeds the duplication threshold for overall code. High duplication increases maintenance effort and can inflate compute and storage overhead on self-hosted SonarQube, complicating analysis and long-term maintainability.
sections.duplication_action: Review the identified repository with the respective project POC to reduce duplication, optimise code efficiency, and improve overall maintainability.
sections.branch_repos_with_inactive_non_main_bran: 132 repositories have non-main branches that have not been scanned for more than 90 days. This represents a significant portion of the portfolio and likely indicates widespread stale or abandoned feature and development branches.
Review these repositories with project POCs to confirm whether non-main branches are still required, and remove or archive stale branches to keep the Sonar environment clean and reports accurate.
sections.branch_repos_with_non_main_branch_count_: Nine repositories have more non-main branches than the agreed threshold, which can clutter scan results and make portfolio-level reporting harder to follow.
Review these repositories with project POCs to consolidate or remove surplus non-main branches and keep the branch count within the agreed threshold.
sections.false_positives_insights: Five repositories contain false positives, with one lacking a justification comment, which limits traceability and auditability of the suppression decisions made.
sections.false_positives_action: Review false-positive markings in the affected repositories to confirm they are valid, and add justification comments where missing to ensure suppression decisions remain auditable.
sections.suppressions_insights: Three repositories have accepted issues, and all three lack justification comments. This weakens auditability and makes it difficult to understand why issues were suppressed.
sections.suppressions_action: Add justification comments to all accepted issues in the three affected repositories so suppression decisions remain auditable and traceable.
sections.quality_gates_insights: Four repositories are using a non-compliant Quality Gate, leading to inconsistent evaluation of code quality conditions across the portfolio.
sections.quality_gates_action: Reassign the affected repositories to the standard Defra Quality Gate to ensure consistent measurement, or confirm the justification for any non-standard Quality Gate with stakeholders and the assurance team.
sections.config_gaps_insights: 11 repositories lack valid scanner configuration, preventing analysis and metric computation for those projects.
sections.config_gaps_action: Fix scanner configuration in the 11 affected repositories to restore analysis coverage and metric computation.
sections.sonarqube_rules_insights: Five custom quality profiles do not inherit from a built-in profile and require manual updates to stay current. Profiles span C#, Java, JavaScript, and TypeScript, with some having not been updated in over two years, increasing the risk of outdated rule coverage.
sections.sonarqube_rules_action: Review each standalone custom profile and either configure it to inherit from the relevant built-in profile, or document a clear justification for maintaining it as standalone to ensure rule coverage remains current.
